growth_records.cite: Rüger M et al. Species-specific viability analysis of Pseudomonas aeruginosa, Burkholderia cepacia and Staphylococcus aureus in mixed culture by flow cytometry. BMC Microbiology (2014). DOI:10.1186/1471-2180-14-56. [PMC3995885]
growth_records.snippet: B. c. pure culture mu_max 0.77 ± 0.02 h-1 (Δt 0-6 h). Gluconate and 2-KDG were produced in pure cultures of P. aeruginosa and B. cepacia ... then consumed by P. aeruginosa and B. cepacia in pure culture.
growth_records.notes: mu_max=0.77 h-1 reported (Table 1). doubling_time=0.693/0.77=0.900 h. B. cepacia produces then re-consumes gluconate/2-KDG. No numeric uptake/secretion rate given.

growth_records.cite: Rüger M et al. Species-specific viability analysis of Pseudomonas aeruginosa, Burkholderia cepacia and Staphylococcus aureus in mixed culture by flow cytometry. BMC Microbiology (2014). DOI:10.1186/1471-2180-14-56. [PMC3995885]
growth_records.snippet: B. c. mixed culture mu_max 0.86 ± 0.09 h-1 (Δt 0-6 h); comparable to pure culture for first 6 h then growth stagnated (competition for glutamine with P. aeruginosa; possible pyocin antagonism).
growth_records.notes: mu_max=0.86 h-1 reported (Table 1, mixed/co-culture). doubling_time=0.693/0.86=0.806 h. Growth stagnation after 6 h attributed to glutamine competition and P. aeruginosa pyocins. No numeric uptake rate.
